_comment: Nemotron-Speech-Streaming en-0.6b per-tensor tolerances for compare_tensors.py.
Reference: NeMo 2.7.2 running fp32 inference via scripts/dump_reference_parakeet_nemo.py.
The dumper preserves the model's NATIVE att_context_style='chunked_limited' (no
--offline-only flag passed). This matches the cache-aware streaming attention mask
the model was trained with; it is the mode that yields the published 2.32% WER
on LibriSpeech test-clean at att_context_size=[70,13] (1.12s chunk).
max_abs  = max(1e-4 × p99_abs, 1e-6)   # 0.01% of typical large value
mean_abs = max(1e-5 × rms,     1e-6)   # 0.001% of RMS energy
These are PROVISIONAL — Stage 4 finalizes them against observed C++ drift.
Entries with _provisional: true make finalization-not-yet-run obvious.
Do NOT ship a model while _provisional entries remain.
Why this variant gets its own tolerances file (not folded into parakeet.json):
the frontend has normalize=none + preemphasis=null, so enc.mel.in is raw log-mel
(min~-16, max~2.5 — un-centered). That cascades to large enc.pre_encode.out magnitudes
(O(1e3)) which would inflate the family-shared parakeet.json budgets for sibling
variants. Per-variant scoping keeps the family tolerances tight.
